devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । तं विलोक्य विनिष्क्रान्तमुज्जिहानमिवोडुपम् । दर्शनीयतमं श्यामं पीतकौशेयवाससम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācataṃ vilokya viniṣkrāntamujjihānamivoḍupamdarśanīyatamaṃ śyāmaṃ pītakauśeyavāsasam
translation: and he begged of them to grant him (the boon of) sleep lasting for a pretty long period inside a cave. When he was granted that boon, the noble-minded king Mucukunda being overcome with fatigue sought from them (undisturbed) sleep.

devanagari: श्रीवत्सवक्षसं भ्राजत्कौस्तुभामुक्तकन्धरम् । पृथुदीर्घचतुर्बाहुं नवकञ्जारुणेक्षणम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīvatsavakṣasaṃ bhrājatkaustubhāmuktakandharampṛthudīrghacaturbāhuṃ navakañjāruṇekṣaṇam
translation:
Vṛndāvana edition adds:
(Mucukunda requested as a boon:) ‘Whoever disturbs my sleep should be instantaneously reduced to ashes, Oh most prominent gods’. The gods granted the boons saying ‘Amen’.

devanagari: नित्यप्रमुदितं श्रीमत्सुकपोलं शुचिस्मितम् । मुखारविन्दं बिभ्राणं स्फुरन्मकरकुण्डलम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: nityapramuditaṃ śrīmatsukapolaṃ śucismitammukhāravindaṃ bibhrāṇaṃ sphuranmakarakuṇḍalam
translation: The Yavana (Kālayavana) perceived Śrī Kṛṣṇa coming out (of the gates of Mathurā) like the rising Moon, most beautiful to look at, dark in complexion, clad in yellow silken garment, with the Śrī-vatsa mark on his bosom, with his neck beautified with the pendant brilliant Kaustubha gem, having four mighty (knee-) long arms and eyes reddish like a fresh-blown lotus, ever beaming with joyousness, with splendid, beautiful cheeks, bright with a smile and lotus-like countenance with dazzling alligator-shaped ear-rings.

devanagari: वासुदेवो ह्ययमिति पुमान्श्रीवत्सलाञ्छनः । चतुर्भुजोऽरविन्दाक्षो वनमाल्यतिसुन्दरः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: vāsudevo hyayamiti pumānśrīvatsalāñchanaḥcaturbhujo'ravindākṣo vanamālyatisundaraḥ
translation: “This exquisitely beautiful man characterised by Śrī-vasta, four arms, lotus-like eyes and wearing a Vanamālā must be Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa). From the characteristic marks described in details by Nārada (I presume) he cannot be anyone else. But since he is armless and walking on foot, I too shall fight with him unarmed (and without using any vehicle)”.

devanagari: लक्षणैर्नारदप्रोक्तैर्नान्यो भवितुमर्हति । निरायुधश्चलन्पद्भ्यां योत्स्येऽनेन निरायुधः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: lakṣaṇairnāradaproktairnānyo bhavitumarhatinirāyudhaścalanpadbhyāṃ yotsye'nena nirāyudhaḥ
translation: “This exquisitely beautiful man characterised by Śrī-vasta, four arms, lotus-like eyes and wearing a Vanamālā must be Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa). From the characteristic marks described in details by Nārada (I presume) he cannot be anyone else. But since he is armless and walking on foot, I too shall fight with him unarmed (and without using any vehicle)”.

devanagari: इति निश्चित्य यवनः प्राद्रवन्तं पराङ्मुखम् । अन्वधावज्जिघृक्षुस्तं दुरापमपि योगिनाम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: iti niścitya yavanaḥ prādravantaṃ parāṅmukhamanvadhāvajjighṛkṣustaṃ durāpamapi yoginām
translation: Resolving thus, the Yavana, with the intention of seizing him whom even the yogin cannot attain with difficulty, chased him who was running fast with his back towards the Yavana.

devanagari: हस्तप्राप्तमिवात्मानं हरिणा स पदे पदे । नीतो दर्शयता दूरं यवनेशोऽद्रिकन्दरम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: hastaprāptamivātmānaṃ hariṇā sa pade padenīto darśayatā dūraṃ yavaneśo'drikandaram
translation: Showing himself to be within his grasp at every step, the king of Yavanas was taken far away to a distant mountain cave.

devanagari: पलायनं यदुकुले जातस्य तव नोचितम् । इति क्षिपन्ननुगतो नैनं प्रापाहताशुभः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: palāyanaṃ yadukule jātasya tava nocitamiti kṣipannanugato nainaṃ prāpāhatāśubhaḥ
translation: “Born as you are in the Yadu race, it is not proper for you to run away from the enemy”. Reproaching him thus while pursuing, he could not overtake him (and catch hold of him) as his inauspicious karmas were not exhausted.

devanagari: एवं क्षिप्तोऽपि भगवान्प्राविशद्गिरिकन्दरम् । सोऽपि प्रविष्टस्तत्रान्यं शयानं ददृशे नरम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ kṣipto'pi bhagavānprāviśadgirikandaramso'pi praviṣṭastatrānyaṃ śayānaṃ dadṛśe naram
translation: Even though he was thus vilified, the Lord entered a deep cave in the mountain[1]. Kālayavana also made his way into it and saw that another person was lying there asleep.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Subodhinī States that the cave is near Dhavalapura (Dholapur) at a distance of to yojanas from Mathurā. N.L. De identified it with a lake three miles to the west of Dholapur and states that on the site of the lake there was formerly a mountain—GDAM I. p. 132.

devanagari: नन्वसौ दूरमानीय शेते मामिह साधुवत् । इति मत्वाच्युतं मूढस्तं पदा समताडयत् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: nanvasau dūramānīya śete māmiha sādhuvatiti matvācyutaṃ mūḍhastaṃ padā samatāḍayat
translation: “Surely after luring me to such a long distance, he is (pretending to be) asleep like an innocent person.” Thinking thus and regarding the sleeping man to be Kṛṣṇa, the stupid fellow kicked him

devanagari: स उत्थाय चिरं सुप्तः शनैरुन्मील्य लोचने । दिशो विलोकयन्पार्श्वे तमद्राक्षीदवस्थितम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: sa utthāya ciraṃ suptaḥ śanairunmīlya locanediśo vilokayanpārśve tamadrākṣīdavasthitam
translation: The man who was asleep for a long time became awakened. He slowly opened his eyes and looked in all directions and espied Kālayavana standing by his side.

devanagari: स तावत्तस्य रुष्टस्य दृष्टिपातेन भारत । देहजेनाग्निना दग्धो भस्मसादभवत्क्षणात् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tāvattasya ruṣṭasya dṛṣṭipātena bhāratadehajenāgninā dagdho bhasmasādabhavatkṣaṇāt
translation: By the angry look of the enraged man (rudely awakened from sleep), there arose a fire out of Kālayavana’s body and he was reduced to ashes in a moment.

devanagari: श्रीराजोवाच । को नाम स पुमान्ब्रह्मन्कस्य किं वीर्य एव च । कस्माद्गुहां गतः शिष्ये किं तेजो यवनार्दनः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrājovācako nāma sa pumānbrahmankasya kiṃ vīrya eva cakasmādguhāṃ gataḥ śiṣye kiṃ tejo yavanārdanaḥ
translation:
King Parīkṣit asked:
What was the name of the man who (thus) killed the Yavana, Oh Brāhmaṇa sage? From whom was he born? What (extra-ordinary) prowess did he possess? Why did he enter the cave for sleep? What majestic lustre did be possess?

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । स इक्ष्वाकुकुले जातो मान्धातृतनयो महान् । मुचुकुन्द इति ख्यातो ब्रह्मण्यः सत्यसङ्गरः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācasa ikṣvākukule jāto māndhātṛtanayo mahānmucukunda iti khyāto brahmaṇyaḥ satyasaṅgaraḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka narrated:
He was born in the race of Ikṣvākus and was a prominent son of Māndhātṛ. He was well-known by the name Mucukunda. He was friendly to Brāhmaṇas and always true to his words.

devanagari: स याचितः सुरगणैरिन्द्राद्यैरात्मरक्षणे । असुरेभ्यः परित्रस्तैस्तद्रक्षां सोऽकरोच्चिरम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa yācitaḥ suragaṇairindrādyairātmarakṣaṇeasurebhyaḥ paritrastaistadrakṣāṃ so'karocciram
translation: He was implored to ensure their protection (from demons) by the host of gods headed by Indra, as they were very much afraid of (and harassed) by Asuras. He accordingly afforded them protection for a long time.

devanagari: लब्ध्वा गुहं ते स्वःपालं मुचुकुन्दमथाब्रुवन् । राजन्विरमतां कृच्छ्राद्भवान्नः परिपालनात् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: labdhvā guhaṃ te svaḥpālaṃ mucukundamathābruvanrājanviramatāṃ kṛcchrādbhavānnaḥ paripālanāt
translation: Having secured (the services of) god Kārttikeya (son of Śiva) as their protector, they spoke to Mucukunda, “Oh King! You may now retire from the onerous task[2] of protecting us.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Though he was in heaven, Mucukunda was always engaged in fighting and had no respite to enjoy heavenly bliss—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: नरलोकं परित्यज्य राज्यं निहतकण्टकम् । अस्मान्पालयतो वीर कामास्ते सर्व उज्झिताः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: naralokaṃ parityajya rājyaṃ nihatakaṇṭakamasmānpālayato vīra kāmāste sarva ujjhitāḥ
translation: Having given up your world kingdom from which all the thorns (miscreants and enemies) were wiped out by you, you have been protecting us in doing which you have given up all pleasures, Oh warrior.

devanagari: सुता महिष्यो भवतो ज्ञातयोऽमात्यमन्त्रिणः । प्रजाश्च तुल्यकालीना नाधुना सन्ति कालिताः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: sutā mahiṣyo bhavato jñātayo'mātyamantriṇaḥprajāśca tulyakālīnā nādhunā santi kālitāḥ
translation: Now none of your sons, queens, clansmen, ministers or your contemporary subjects survives as they are subjected to the influence of Time (and have died).

devanagari: कालो बलीयान्बलिनां भगवानीश्वरोऽव्ययः । प्रजाः कालयते क्रीडन्पशुपालो यथा पशून् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: kālo balīyānbalināṃ bhagavānīśvaro'vyayaḥprajāḥ kālayate krīḍanpaśupālo yathā paśūn
translation: The glorious supreme immortal Lord, the Timespirit is mightier than the mighty. He drives off all creatures easily as in a sport, as a herdsman would do his cattle.

devanagari: वरं वृणीष्व भद्रं ते ऋते कैवल्यमद्य नः । एक एवेश्वरस्तस्य भगवान्विष्णुरव्ययः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: varaṃ vṛṇīṣva bhadraṃ te ṛte kaivalyamadya naḥeka eveśvarastasya bhagavānviṣṇuravyayaḥ
translation: May God bless you. Please ask of us any boon except the final emancipation. For the imperishable Almighty Lord Visṇu alone has the capability to grant it.

devanagari: एवमुक्तः स वै देवानभिवन्द्य महायशाः । अशयिष्ट गुहाविष्टो निद्रया देवदत्तया ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: evamuktaḥ sa vai devānabhivandya mahāyaśāḥaśayiṣṭa guhāviṣṭo nidrayā devadattayā
translation:
Vṛndāvana edition adds:
(Mucukunda requested as a boon:) ‘Whoever disturbs my sleep should be instantaneously reduced to ashes, Oh most prominent gods’. The gods granted the boons saying ‘Amen’.

devanagari: यवने भस्मसान्नीते भगवान्सात्वतर्षभः । आत्मानं दर्शयामास मुचुकुन्दाय धीमते ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: yavane bhasmasānnīte bhagavānsātvatarṣabhaḥātmānaṃ darśayāmāsa mucukundāya dhīmate
translation: He entered a cave (shown to him by gods) and reposed himself to enjoy the sleep granted to him by gods.

devanagari: तमालोक्य घनश्यामं पीतकौशेयवाससम् । श्रीवत्सवक्षसं भ्राजत्कौस्तुभेन विराजितम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: tamālokya ghanaśyāmaṃ pītakauśeyavāsasamśrīvatsavakṣasaṃ bhrājatkaustubhena virājitam
translation: (The gods assured him) “He who would wake you up thoughtlessly while you are asleep, (and thus disturb your rest), the stupid fellow be reduced to ashes the very moment he is woked at by you”.

devanagari: चतुर्भुजं रोचमानं वैजयन्त्या च मालया । चारुप्रसन्नवदनं स्फुरन्मकरकुण्डलम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: caturbhujaṃ rocamānaṃ vaijayantyā ca mālayācāruprasannavadanaṃ sphuranmakarakuṇḍalam
translation: When the Yavana was reduced to ashes, the glorious Lord Kṛsṇa, the most prominent Sātvata manifested himself to the intelligent king Mucukunḍa.

devanagari: प्रेक्षणीयं नृलोकस्य सानुरागस्मितेक्षणम् । अपीच्यवयसं मत्तमृगेन्द्रोदारविक्रमम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: prekṣaṇīyaṃ nṛlokasya sānurāgasmitekṣaṇamapīcyavayasaṃ mattamṛgendrodāravikramam
translation: He perceived the Lord, of dark-blue complexion like a cloud, wearing a yellow silken garment, with the Śrīvatsa mark on the chest, lustrous with the refulgent Kaustubha gem, having four arms, appearing resplendent with the Vanamālā (a garland of forest flowers special to Śrī Kṛṣṇa), with a charming, kindly disposed countenance, beautified with radiant alligatorshaped ear-rings, captivating to the eyes of all human beings[4] with smiling looks expressing gracious love, endowed with youthfulness, noble in gait like a proud lion.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
v.l. enhanting to the eyes of the three worlds (Padaratnāvalī)

devanagari: पर्यपृच्छन्महाबुद्धिस्तेजसा तस्य धर्षितः । शङ्कितः शनकै राजा दुर्धर्षमिव तेजसा ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: paryapṛcchanmahābuddhistejasā tasya dharṣitaḥśaṅkitaḥ śanakai rājā durdharṣamiva tejasā
translation: He perceived the Lord, of dark-blue complexion like a cloud, wearing a yellow silken garment, with the Śrīvatsa mark on the chest, lustrous with the refulgent Kaustubha gem, having four arms, appearing resplendent with the Vanamālā (a garland of forest flowers special to Śrī Kṛṣṇa), with a charming, kindly disposed countenance, beautified with radiant alligatorshaped ear-rings, captivating to the eyes of all human beings[4] with smiling looks expressing gracious love, endowed with youthfulness, noble in gait like a proud lion.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
v.l. enhanting to the eyes of the three worlds (Padaratnāvalī)

devanagari: श्रीमुचुकुन्द उवाच । को भवानिह सम्प्राप्तो विपिने गिरिगह्वरे । पद्भ्यां पद्मपलाशाभ्यां विचरस्युरुकण्टके ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīmucukunda uvācako bhavāniha samprāpto vipine girigahvarepadbhyāṃ padmapalāśābhyāṃ vicarasyurukaṇṭake
translation: He perceived the Lord, of dark-blue complexion like a cloud, wearing a yellow silken garment, with the Śrīvatsa mark on the chest, lustrous with the refulgent Kaustubha gem, having four arms, appearing resplendent with the Vanamālā (a garland of forest flowers special to Śrī Kṛṣṇa), with a charming, kindly disposed countenance, beautified with radiant alligatorshaped ear-rings, captivating to the eyes of all human beings[4] with smiling looks expressing gracious love, endowed with youthfulness, noble in gait like a proud lion.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
v.l. enhanting to the eyes of the three worlds (Padaratnāvalī)

devanagari: किं स्वित्तेजस्विनां तेजो भगवान्वा विभावसुः । सूर्यः सोमो महेन्द्रो वा लोकपालो परोऽपि वा ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ svittejasvināṃ tejo bhagavānvā vibhāvasuḥsūryaḥ somo mahendro vā lokapālo paro'pi vā
translation: Being over-awed with his dazzling effulgence, the highly intelligent and wise king gently enquired of him as he was unapproachable due to his splendour.

devanagari: मन्ये त्वां देवदेवानां त्रयाणां पुरुषर्षभम् । यद्बाधसे गुहाध्वान्तं प्रदीपः प्रभया यथा ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: manye tvāṃ devadevānāṃ trayāṇāṃ puruṣarṣabhamyadbādhase guhādhvāntaṃ pradīpaḥ prabhayā yathā
translation:
Mucukunda said
“Who are you arrived here in this inaccessible mountain cave in a dense forest? How is it that you go about in this forest full of thorns, on foot tender like lotus-petals?

devanagari: शुश्रूषतामव्यलीकमस्माकं नरपुङ्गव । स्वजन्म कर्म गोत्रं वा कथ्यतां यदि रोचते ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: śuśrūṣatāmavyalīkamasmākaṃ narapuṅgavasvajanma karma gotraṃ vā kathyatāṃ yadi rocate
translation: You are the embodiment of majestic lustre of all those endowed with effulgence and luminosity. Are you the glorious Fire-god or the Sun-god or the Moon-god or the great Indra or the guardian deity of the world or any other divinity of that high status?

devanagari: वयं तु पुरुषव्याघ्र ऐक्ष्वाकाः क्षत्रबन्धवः । मुचुकुन्द इति प्रोक्तो यौवनाश्वात्मजः प्रभो ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: vayaṃ tu puruṣavyāghra aikṣvākāḥ kṣatrabandhavaḥmucukunda iti prokto yauvanāśvātmajaḥ prabho
translation: I regard you as the Supreme Person, Lord Viṣṇu out of the three Gods of gods (viz. Brahmā, Viṣṇu and Śiva) as you dispel the darkness of the cave (or remove ignorance in my heart) by your splendour as does the light of a lamp.

devanagari: चिरप्रजागरश्रान्तो निद्रयापहतेन्द्रियः । शयेऽस्मिन्विजने कामं केनाप्युत्थापितोऽधुना ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: ciraprajāgaraśrānto nidrayāpahatendriyaḥśaye'sminvijane kāmaṃ kenāpyutthāpito'dhunā
translation: Oh most excellent person! If it pleases You, may your birth, exploits, family name be frankly disclosed to us who are sincerely desirous of hearing it (or who sincerely desire to render you service).

devanagari: सोऽपि भस्मीकृतो नूनमात्मीयेनैव पाप्मना । अनन्तरं भवान्श्रीमांल्लक्षितोऽमित्रशासनः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: so'pi bhasmīkṛto nūnamātmīyenaiva pāpmanāanantaraṃ bhavānśrīmāṃllakṣito'mitraśāsanaḥ
translation: As for us we are Kṣattriyas of the Ikṣvāku race, Oh Tiger among men. I am, Oh Lord, known as Mucukunda, the son of Māndhātṛ (who was the son of Yuvanāśva).

devanagari: तेजसा तेऽविषह्येण भूरि द्रष्टुं न शक्नुमः । हतौजसा महाभाग माननीयोऽसि देहिनाम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: tejasā te'viṣahyeṇa bhūri draṣṭuṃ na śaknumaḥhataujasā mahābhāga mānanīyo'si dehinām
translation: Completely exhausted by vigil extending over a long period and with all the limbs of my body benumbed and overpowered with sleep, I lay enjoying un-interrupted sleep to my heart’s content, from which I was rudely awakened by some unknown person, just now.

devanagari: एवं सम्भाषितो राज्ञा भगवान्भूतभावनः । प्रत्याह प्रहसन्वाण्या मेघनादगभीरया ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ sambhāṣito rājñā bhagavānbhūtabhāvanaḥpratyāha prahasanvāṇyā meghanādagabhīrayā
translation: Even he has been reduced to ashes, verily by his own sins, indeed. It is after that your glorious personality, the destroyer of enemies, has been perceived by me.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । जन्मकर्माभिधानानि सन्ति मेऽङ्ग सहस्रशः । न शक्यन्तेऽनुसङ्ख्यातुमनन्तत्वान्मयापि हि ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvācajanmakarmābhidhānāni santi me'ṅga sahasraśaḥna śakyante'nusaṅkhyātumanantatvānmayāpi hi
translation: Being bedazzled by your unbearable lustre, we caṇnot look at you for long. Our powers are ineffective in your presence, Oh blessed God. But you deserve to be honoured by all embodied beings (This much we can say about you)”.

devanagari: क्वचिद्रजांसि विममे पार्थिवान्युरुजन्मभिः । गुणकर्माभिधानानि न मे जन्मानि कर्हिचित् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacidrajāṃsi vimame pārthivānyurujanmabhiḥguṇakarmābhidhānāni na me janmāni karhicit
translation: Thus respectfully addressed by the king, the glorious Lord, the Creator of all beings, laughed aloud and replied to him in a voice deep like the thundering of a cloud.

devanagari: कालत्रयोपपन्नानि जन्मकर्माणि मे नृप । अनुक्रमन्तो नैवान्तं गच्छन्ति परमर्षयः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: kālatrayopapannāni janmakarmāṇi me nṛpaanukramanto naivāntaṃ gacchanti paramarṣayaḥ
translation:
The Almighty Lord replied:
Dear King! my incarnations, exploits and epithets are innumerable. They, being infinite in number, it is not possible even for me to count them.

devanagari: तथाप्यद्यतनान्यङ्ग शृणुष्व गदतो मम । विज्ञापितो विरिञ्चेन पुराहं धर्मगुप्तये ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: tathāpyadyatanānyaṅga śṛṇuṣva gadato mamavijñāpito viriñcena purāhaṃ dharmaguptaye
translation: Possibly someone might reckon, through (a continuous period of) many lives, all the particles of dust over the earth, but nobody can ever enumerate my excellences, exploits, designations and incarnations.[5]
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Cf. viṣṇor nu kaṃ vīryāṃ pravocaṃ yaḥ pārthivāni vimame rajāṃsi /—Ṛg Veda Saṃhitā. 1.154.1, AV 7.26.1; VS. 5-18, TS. 1.2.13.3

devanagari: भूमेर्भारायमाणानामसुराणां क्षयाय च । अवतीर्णो यदुकुले गृह आनकदुन्दुभेः । वदन्ति वासुदेवेति वसुदेवसुतं हि माम् ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: bhūmerbhārāyamāṇānāmasurāṇāṃ kṣayāya caavatīrṇo yadukule gṛha ānakadundubheḥvadanti vāsudeveti vasudevasutaṃ hi mām
translation: Even great sages (like Parāśara, Vaiśampāyana etc.) who tried to describe my descent and exploits pertaining to the past, present and future in order, have not been able to reach the end.

devanagari: कालनेमिर्हतः कंसः प्रलम्बाद्याश्च सद्द्विषः । अयं च यवनो दग्धो राजंस्ते तिग्मचक्षुषा ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: kālanemirhataḥ kaṃsaḥ pralambādyāśca saddviṣaḥayaṃ ca yavano dagdho rājaṃste tigmacakṣuṣā
translation: However, listen to me as I narrate those pertaining to the present time, Oh dear king. Formerly, I was entreated by god Brahmā to protect the order of righteousness and for annihilation of Asuras who caused a burden to the earth.

devanagari: सोऽहं तवानुग्रहार्थं गुहामेतामुपागतः । प्रार्थितः प्रचुरं पूर्वं त्वयाहं भक्तवत्सलः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: so'haṃ tavānugrahārthaṃ guhāmetāmupāgataḥprārthitaḥ pracuraṃ pūrvaṃ tvayāhaṃ bhaktavatsalaḥ
translation: I have descended in the race of Yadus in the house of Vasudeva. As I am born of Vasudeva, they call me Vāsudeva.

devanagari: वरान्वृणीष्व राजर्षे सर्वान्कामान्ददामि ते । मां प्रसन्नो जनः कश्चिन्न भूयोऽर्हति शोचितुम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: varānvṛṇīṣva rājarṣe sarvānkāmāndadāmi temāṃ prasanno janaḥ kaścinna bhūyo'rhati śocitum
translation: Kālanemi, born as Kaṃsa and the haters of the righteous like Pralamba and others have been killed by me. This Yavana is caused to be burnt by me through your fiery looks.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्युक्तस्तं प्रणम्याह मुचुकुन्दो मुदान्वितः । ज्ञात्वा नारायणं देवं गर्गवाक्यमनुस्मरन् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaityuktastaṃ praṇamyāha mucukundo mudānvitaḥjñātvā nārāyaṇaṃ devaṃ gargavākyamanusmaran
translation: Having been earnestly and repeatedly implored by you in days gone by, I, that Vāsudeva, being fond of my votaries, have come over to this cave for showering my divine Grace on you.

devanagari: श्रीमुचुकुन्द उवाच । विमोहितोऽयं जन ईश मायया त्वदीयया त्वां न भजत्यनर्थदृक् । सुखाय दुःखप्रभवेषु सज्जते गृहेषु योषित्पुरुषश्च वञ्चितः ...
english_devnagari: śrīmucukunda uvācavimohito'yaṃ jana īśa māyayā tvadīyayā tvāṃ na bhajatyanarthadṛksukhāya duḥkhaprabhaveṣu sajjate gṛheṣu yoṣitpuruṣaśca vañcitaḥ
translation: Seek boons from me, Oh royal sage. I shall bestow on you all desired objects as no person that resorts to me (submitting himself completely to my Will) shall suffer misery any more.

devanagari: लब्ध्वा जनो दुर्लभमत्र मानुषं । कथञ्चिदव्यङ्गमयत्नतोऽनघ । पादारविन्दं न भजत्यसन्मतिर् । गृहान्धकूपे पतितो यथा पशुः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: labdhvā jano durlabhamatra mānuṣaṃkathañcidavyaṅgamayatnato'naghapādāravindaṃ na bhajatyasanmatirgṛhāndhakūpe patito yathā paśuḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
When addressed thus, Mucukunda bowed down to him. Remembering the words of Vṛddha Garga (predicting God Viṣṇu’s revelation to him), he realized that he (Vāsudeva) was Lord Nārāyaṇa himself. Overwhelmed with joy, he submitted as follows:

devanagari: ममैष कालोऽजित निष्फलो गतो राज्यश्रियोन्नद्धमदस्य भूपतेः । मर्त्यात्मबुद्धेः सुतदारकोशभूष्वासज्जमानस्य दुरन्तचिन्तया ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: mamaiṣa kālo'jita niṣphalo gato rājyaśriyonnaddhamadasya bhūpateḥmartyātmabuddheḥ sutadārakośabhūṣvāsajjamānasya durantacintayā
translation:
Mucukunda said:
Oh Lord! This person (i.e. I or human being) whether male or female is absolutely deluded by your Māyā (Deluding Potency). With his eye (or mind) concentrated on saṃsāra (Or being unable to realize that you alone are the Reality), he does not seek resort in you (as a devotee). For the sake of happiness, he is attached to the house including wife, children, wealth etc.) which is the source of all miseries and is thus deceived (in his search for happiness).

devanagari: कलेवरेऽस्मिन्घटकुड्यसन्निभे । निरूढमानो नरदेव इत्यहम् । वृतो रथेभाश्वपदात्यनीकपैर् । गां पर्यटंस्त्वागणयन्सुदुर्मदः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: kalevare'sminghaṭakuḍyasannibhenirūḍhamāno naradeva ityahamvṛto rathebhāśvapadātyanīkapairgāṃ paryaṭaṃstvāgaṇayansudurmadaḥ
translation: After securing the blessing of birth as a human being in this world—which, in itself, is very difficult to obtain—and that too with proportionate limbs (and soundness of mind) without any efforts (on one’s part but through Divine Grace), a person who does not resort to the lotus-feet of the Lord, is, Oh handsome Lord, a wicked-minded (dull-witted) fellow who, like a beast desirous of eating grass falls into a grass-covered well (with no way out), sinks down in the dark deep well of domestic life.

devanagari: प्रमत्तमुच्चैरितिकृत्यचिन्तया प्रवृद्धलोभं विषयेषु लालसम् । त्वमप्रमत्तः सहसाभिपद्यसे क्षुल्लेलिहानोऽहिरिवाखुमन्तकः ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: pramattamuccairitikṛtyacintayā pravṛddhalobhaṃ viṣayeṣu lālasamtvamapramattaḥ sahasābhipadyase kṣullelihāno'hirivākhumantakaḥ
translation: All this life of mine has been fruitlessly wasted, Oh Invincible Almighty, for I behaved as a king elated with the pride of affluence and splendour of my royalty, identifying this mortal body as the Self and in endless anxiety due to my attachment to sons, wives, treasures, and lands.

devanagari: पुरा रथैर्हेमपरिष्कृतैश्चरन् । मतंगजैर्वा नरदेवसंज्ञितः । स एव कालेन दुरत्ययेन ते । कलेवरो विट्कृमिभस्मसंज्ञितः ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: purā rathairhemapariṣkṛtaiścaranmataṃgajairvā naradevasaṃjñitaḥsa eva kālena duratyayena tekalevaro viṭkṛmibhasmasaṃjñitaḥ
translation: Forgetting you altogether, I became incorrigibly haughty and lorḍly with the deep-rooted (ignorant) attachment to this inert body which is similar to an earthen pot or a wall (and thus totally different from the Soul), the inherent pride of being a god among men (king) surrounded by many military officers commanding division of chariots, elephants, cavalry-men and foot-soldiers and leading expeditions over the earth.

devanagari: निर्जित्य दिक्चक्रमभूतविग्रहो वरासनस्थः समराजवन्दितः । गृहेषु मैथुन्यसुखेषु योषितां क्रीडामृगः पूरुष ईश नीयते ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: nirjitya dikcakramabhūtavigraho varāsanasthaḥ samarājavanditaḥgṛheṣu maithunyasukheṣu yoṣitāṃ krīḍāmṛgaḥ pūruṣa īśa nīyate
translation: Like unto a hungry serpent, licking the corners of its mouth with its tongue, seizing upon and swallowing up a rat, you, the alert God of Death, surprisingly overtake a person who forgets you altogether, and is engrossed in pondering over the ways and means of achieving worldly plans which (even if frustrated) intensify the desire for worldly objects which (even after attainment) extremely increases the greed and thirst for them.

devanagari: करोति कर्माणि तपःसुनिष्ठितो निवृत्तभोगस्तदपेक्षयाददत् । पुनश्च भूयासमहं स्वराडिति प्रवृद्धतर्षो न सुखाय कल्पते ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: karoti karmāṇi tapaḥsuniṣṭhito nivṛttabhogastadapekṣayādadatpunaśca bhūyāsamahaṃ svarāḍiti pravṛddhatarṣo na sukhāya kalpate
translation: The mortal body which formerly moved in chariots plated with gold or rode on elephants and was designated (honorifically) as ‘the god among men’, is reduced to what may be called as excrement (if devoured after death by dogs, crows etc. in an undisposed condition), as worms (if interred in the grave) and as ashes (if cremated by fire) during the inevitable course of Time.

devanagari: भवापवर्गो भ्रमतो यदा भवेज्जनस्य तर्ह्यच्युत सत्समागमः । सत्सङ्गमो यर्हि तदैव सद्गतौ परावरेशे त्वयि जायते मतिः ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: bhavāpavargo bhramato yadā bhavejjanasya tarhyacyuta satsamāgamaḥsatsaṅgamo yarhi tadaiva sadgatau parāvareśe tvayi jāyate matiḥ
translation: Having conquered (all kingdoms spread over the earth in) all directions, with the (possibility of future) wars forestalled, occupying the exalted imperial throne, being paid respects by his (former) equals, the man is reduced to the state of a pet (a beast for play) for women in his home which is characterized by sexual pleasures, Oh Almighty Lord.

devanagari: मन्ये ममानुग्रह ईश ते कृतो राज्यानुबन्धापगमो यदृच्छया । यः प्रार्थ्यते साधुभिरेकचर्यया वनं विविक्षद्भिरखण्डभूमिपैः ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: manye mamānugraha īśa te kṛto rājyānubandhāpagamo yadṛcchayāyaḥ prārthyate sādhubhirekacaryayā vanaṃ vivikṣadbhirakhaṇḍabhūmipaiḥ
translation: Being devoted to the performance of austerities denying himself all pleasures of senses, he continues to perform righteous acts and donate gifts (in the present life) with the expectant desire, “I should become the king of heaven (Indra) or the sole sovereign ruler of the world. But this simply intensifies his thirst and is not conducive to real happiness.

devanagari: न कामयेऽन्यं तव पादसेवनादकिञ्चनप्रार्थ्यतमाद्वरं विभो । आराध्य कस्त्वां ह्यपवर्गदं हरे वृणीत आर्यो वरमात्मबन्धनम् ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: na kāmaye'nyaṃ tava pādasevanādakiñcanaprārthyatamādvaraṃ vibhoārādhya kastvāṃ hyapavargadaṃ hare vṛṇīta āryo varamātmabandhanam
translation: When the time of release from the saṃsāra of a person wandering in the cycle of births and deaths, approaches then only companionship with saintly persons takes place, Oh Imperishable Lord. It is only after association with saints that the mind (being averse to attachment to all worldly objects turns to your devotion—you who are the Master of the high and the low.

devanagari: तस्माद्विसृज्याशिष ईश सर्वतो रजस्तमःसत्त्वगुणानुबन्धनाः । निरञ्जनं निर्गुणमद्वयं परं त्वां ज्ञाप्तिमात्रं पुरुषं व्रजाम्यहम् ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmādvisṛjyāśiṣa īśa sarvato rajastamaḥsattvaguṇānubandhanāḥnirañjanaṃ nirguṇamadvayaṃ paraṃ tvāṃ jñāptimātraṃ puruṣaṃ vrajāmyaham
translation: That the bondage of attachment to kingship should be severed automatically (without any efforts on my part) is, Me-thinks your special divine Grace shown to me—the Grace devoutly solicited by saints of unflinching devotion to you and by kings who though they ruled over the entire earth, still wished to retire to the forest for penance.

devanagari: चिरमिह वृजिनार्तस्तप्यमानोऽनुतापैर् । अवितृषषडमित्रोऽलब्धशान्तिः कथञ्चित् । शरणद समुपेतस्त्वत्पदाब्जं परात्मन् । अभयमृतमशोकं पाहि मापन...
english_devnagari: ciramiha vṛjinārtastapyamāno'nutāpairavitṛṣaṣaḍamitro'labdhaśāntiḥ kathañcitśaraṇada samupetastvatpadābjaṃ parātmanabhayamṛtamaśokaṃ pāhi māpannamīśa
translation: Oh Omnipresent Lord! I do not seek any other boon from you except rendering service to your feet, which is regarded as the most covetable blessing according to those who have renounced everything (and possess nothing of their own). Having propitiated you, the bestower of Mokṣa (Liberation), Oh Hari, what wise man would solicit a boon from you which, will create a bondage to one’s soul.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । सार्वभौम महाराज मतिस्ते विमलोर्जिता । वरैः प्रलोभितस्यापि न कामैर्विहता यतः ॥ ५८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvācasārvabhauma mahārāja matiste vimalorjitāvaraiḥ pralobhitasyāpi na kāmairvihatā yataḥ
translation: Hence, rejecting all blessings which are bound with the guṇas viz. Sattva, rajas and tamas, Oh Lord, I seek resort in you, the Supreme Person, the very embodiment of knowledge and consciousness unsullied by any taint, transcendent to all guṇaṣ, the One without second.

devanagari: प्रलोभितो वरैर्यत्त्वमप्रमादाय विद्धि तत् । न धीरेकान्तभक्तानामाशीर्भिर्भिद्यते क्वचित् ॥ ५९ ॥
english_devnagari: pralobhito varairyattvamapramādāya viddhi tatna dhīrekāntabhaktānāmāśīrbhirbhidyate kvacit
translation: For a very long time, I have been troubled in this world with fruits of action and have been afflicted with the vāsanās (effects left on the mind after enjoying fruits of actions) and am harrassed by six unsatiable (internal) enemies. Being unable to secure mental peace, I have with great difficulty approached your lotus-feet Oh Lord who are the Supreme Souḥ free from fear and grief of death, Oh Lord! Be pleased to protect me who am full of distress.

devanagari: युञ्जानानामभक्तानां प्राणायामादिभिर्मनः । अक्षीणवासनं राजन्दृश्यते पुनरुत्थितम् ॥ ६० ॥
english_devnagari: yuñjānānāmabhaktānāṃ prāṇāyāmādibhirmanaḥakṣīṇavāsanaṃ rājandṛśyate punarutthitam
translation:
The glorious Lord said:
Oh Emperor of the entire world! Oh great king, Your mind is free from impurities (like attachment, greed eted and judgment clear. For though you were tempted with boons (at your sweet will), you were not lured thereby.

devanagari: विचरस्व महीं कामं मय्यावेशितमानसः । अस्त्वेवं नित्यदा तुभ्यं भक्तिर्मय्यनपायिनी ॥ ६१ ॥
english_devnagari: vicarasva mahīṃ kāmaṃ mayyāveśitamānasaḥastvevaṃ nityadā tubhyaṃ bhaktirmayyanapāyinī
translation: It was with a view to test your unerring, vigilance that you were tempted with boons. Please know that the mind and intelligence of unflinching devotees is never changed by temptation of blessings.

devanagari: क्षात्रधर्मस्थितो जन्तून्न्यवधीर्मृगयादिभिः । समाहितस्तत्तपसा जह्यघं मदुपाश्रितः ॥ ६२ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣātradharmasthito jantūnnyavadhīrmṛgayādibhiḥsamāhitastattapasā jahyaghaṃ madupāśritaḥ
translation: The mind of those who are other than devotees and who control it by Prāṇāyāma (breath control) and other means, is not devoid of vāsanās (the subtle desires) and it is noticed that they are again attached to sense-objects.

devanagari: जन्मन्यनन्तरे राजन्सर्वभूतसुहृत्तमः । भूत्वा द्विजवरस्त्वं वै मामुपैष्यसि केवलम् ॥ ६३ ॥
english_devnagari: janmanyanantare rājansarvabhūtasuhṛttamaḥbhūtvā dvijavarastvaṃ vai māmupaiṣyasi kevalam
translation: You may roam over the earth at will after setting your mind on me. May your devotion unto me be everlasting and unflinching for ever.
